metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify which eval seam the db-migrations overlay eval hooks into (e.g. a new `code-review-<overlay>` dispatch branch in eval_solver, or a standalone runner entry point), so AC2 ('passing recall + no-fire eval') is executable and not just an authoring intent.
findings.finding: AC2 ('lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval') presupposes an eval harness for code-review overlays that does not exist in the codebase: eval_solver.run_case has no dispatch for code-review prompt ids, no packaged eval_specs/*.eval.yaml covers any code-review overlay, and no seam is documented for wiring such a spec. The plan does not specify how the eval is authored, which solver path it uses, or how it is plugged into the existing eval infrastructure — making AC2 unverifiable as written.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — AC2 ('Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `## Files` entry for the eval wiring (e.g. extending eval_solver.run_case with a code-review overlay arm that feeds the diff context + calls the overlay prompt), or explicitly scope AC2 to 'spec structure passes validate_eval_spec offline' if live execution is deferred, with a note in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the concrete no-fire (FP-guard) conditions the overlay must carry — at minimum: non-persisted/in-memory changes, test-only migration fixtures, and changes that touch only migration framework config — so the AC's 'does not over-suppress' clause is verifiable.
findings.finding: The plan promises FP-guard (no-fire) content but does not enumerate which conditions should suppress the overlay from firing, leaving the FP-discrimination design unspecified and making the 'does not over-suppress valid patterns' AC unverifiable at review time.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; does not over-suppress valid patterns'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the FP-discrimination guard conditions in the plan (e.g., 'safe migration with explicit down() step → do not fire destructive pattern'; 'nullable add with no backfill → do not fire backfill-correctness pattern') so the overlay author has a concrete, plan-specified guard vocabulary.
findings.finding: The FP-discrimination guards ('both halves') are referenced as a required overlay component but neither half is defined anywhere in the plan — the executor must invent the guard list from scratch.
findings.location: What section: 'FP-guards (both halves)'; AC1: 'db-migrations overlay carries both recall patterns and FP-discrimination guards'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the minimum fixture set for the eval: enumerate at least one recall fixture and one no-fire fixture per named pattern (delete+replace migration symmetry, destructive migrations, backfill correctness, expand/contract sequencing, lock duration), so the executor has no design choice to make.
findings.finding: The eval spec and fixtures deliverable is under-specified: the plan names it as a file artifact but does not define how many recall cases are required, which specific migration scenarios each fixture must cover, or how many no-fire (FP-guard) cases are needed. This defers the design of the eval suite to the executor.
findings.location: Files section: 'eval spec + fixtures for the db-migrations overlay (recall + no-fire cases)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Files section or a new 'Eval Coverage' subsection: 'Eval must include: (a) 5 recall fixtures — one per named pattern; (b) ≥3 no-fire fixtures covering common FP triggers (e.g., additive schema changes, non-data-loss rename with explicit backfill); threshold: 100% recall, 0 FP fires on no-fire set.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate at least the major no-fire (FP-guard) scenarios in the plan (e.g., additive-only migration, migration with explicit lock-timeout config, backfill behind feature flag) so the eval fixture set has a specified coverage target.
findings.finding: The no-fire (FP-guard) eval cases are not enumerated, leaving the observable proof for 'does not over-suppress valid patterns' underspecified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2; Files section — 'eval spec + fixtures for the db-migrations overlay (recall + no-fire cases)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet list under the Files section or AC2 naming 3–5 concrete no-fire cases that mirror the 5 recall patterns, e.g.: (1) plain additive column addition — should NOT fire destructive-migration; (2) migration with explicit `lock_timeout` set — should NOT fire lock-duration; (3) expand step of an expand/contract pair — should NOT fire for missing contract when contract is a later migration in the same PR.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit recall patterns for (c) resumability/idempotency and (d) dual-write window to the overlay's enrichment scope, alongside the five already named.
findings.finding: The plan names five recall patterns but omits the dual-write window (rows written DURING the migration) and resumability/idempotency as explicit recall targets, leaving T11(c) and T11(d) uncovered by the enrichment specification.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the 'What' section to include 'resumability/idempotency (migration re-runnable without double-applying)' and 'dual-write window (concurrent writes during backfill handled)' as named recall targets, and provide matching FP-guard language (e.g., 'do not flag if the migration framework guarantees idempotency natively').

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a concrete proving command and numeric threshold for AC2 (e.g., 'run `make eval-db-migrations`; expect recall 5/5, FP-fires 0/3') so done-ness is objectively verifiable in-session.
findings.finding: AC2 ('Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval') lacks a concrete proving command and a defined numeric threshold, making done-ness unverifiable — 'passing' is subjective without a target score.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; does not over-suppress valid patterns.'
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC2 as: 'Eval harness (`make eval-db-migrations` or equivalent) reports recall N/N on recall fixtures and 0 fires on no-fire fixtures; command exits 0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and link the concrete artifact that 'dso content' refers to (e.g., a specific file path, external URL, or inline enumeration of the db-migrations recall patterns and FP-guards) so the implementing agent has a verifiable source to port from.
findings.finding: The plan invokes 'dso content' as the source material for enriching the overlay, but 'dso' does not resolve to any locatable file, module, ADR, or document in this repository containing a db-migrations rubric. Repo-wide search finds 'dso' only as: an external orchestration system referenced in experiment harnesses, a corpus label in eval fixtures, and Jira-sync docs. An implementing agent cannot locate and port 'dso content' without knowing what artifact is meant.
findings.location: What section: 'Enrich the db-migrations overlay prompt with dso content'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'dso content' with an explicit reference: either a file path within the repo (e.g., `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/dso-criteria-specs.md` if that is the source), an external URL, or simply inline the authoritative pattern list directly in the plan's What section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'backfill batching/throttling' as an explicit recall pattern distinct from 'backfill correctness' so T11(b) (large backfills are batched, not one giant transaction) is independently covered.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify batching/throttling (T11(b)) as a named recall pattern; 'backfill correctness' is named but correctness and batching are orthogonal — a semantically correct but single-transaction giant backfill satisfies correctness yet fails T11(b).
findings.suggested_fix: Rename or split the 'backfill correctness' pattern into (i) semantic correctness (right rows, right values) and (ii) execution safety (batched/throttled, not a single giant transaction), adding an example pattern for detecting unbatched UPDATE … WHERE id > 0.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the eval artifact's file path, required scorer types (at minimum: a recall scorer and a no-fire/FP scorer), numeric pass threshold (coverage_threshold or gate), and the minimum number of labeled fixture cases for both recall and no-fire axes.
findings.finding: The eval spec + fixtures deliverable has no specified file path, no defined scorer/gate structure, and no enumerated fixture scenarios, leaving the artifact's required shape entirely undefined.
findings.location: Files section: 'eval spec + fixtures for the db-migrations overlay (recall + no-fire cases)'; AC2: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Files section: `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-db-migrations.eval.yaml` and add to the What section or a new Spec section: the eval must include ≥N recall cases (one per named pattern) and ≥M no-fire cases (one per FP-guard), with a `coverage_threshold` and an explicit `gate` expression matching the pattern of existing evals.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand AC1 to explicitly enumerate (or reference) each of the five named recall patterns so completeness is verifiable, e.g. 'overlay includes recall patterns for: delete+replace symmetry, destructive migrations, backfill correctness, expand/contract sequencing, and lock duration — each with a corresponding FP-guard.'
findings.finding: The 'What' section names five specific recall patterns (delete+replace migration symmetry, destructive migrations, backfill correctness, expand/contract sequencing, lock duration), but AC1 only generically requires 'both recall patterns and FP-discrimination guards' — no criterion individually verifies each named pattern landed in the overlay. An implementer could omit one pattern and still pass AC1.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1; cross-referenced with 'What' section
findings.suggested_fix: Update AC1 to: 'db-migrations overlay carries recall patterns for all five named domains (delete+replace migration symmetry, destructive migrations, backfill correctness, expand/contract sequencing, lock duration) AND corresponding FP-discrimination guards for each.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and cite the concrete source for the 'dso content' db-migrations rubric (a specific file path in the external DSO project or an equivalent internal reference) so the recall patterns and FP-guards can be faithfully transcribed rather than invented.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Enrich the db-migrations overlay prompt with dso content' treating 'dso content' as a known, locatable source, but no db-migrations-specific DSO rubric file exists anywhere in this repo. The external DSO project (~/digital-service-orchestra) that originated the plan-review criterion specs is not present in the repo, and the five named recall patterns are not traced to any verifiable source file, leaving the primary content input unresolved.
findings.location: What section — 'Enrich the db-migrations overlay prompt with dso content'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `## Source` section naming the authoritative DSO file (e.g. `~/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/db-migrations.md` or equivalent) from which the recall patterns and FP-guards will be drawn, mirroring how `dso-criteria-specs.md` cites `~/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/` for plan-review criteria.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and either inline or link (as a repo-resolvable path) the 'dso content' source that supplies the db-migrations recall patterns and FP-guard vocabulary, so the plan is self-contained.
findings.finding: 'dso content' is referenced as the authoritative source for enriching the overlay, but no artifact named, documented, or resolvable as 'dso' containing db-migrations rubric exists anywhere in the codebase. An executor has no locatable source from which to draw the enrichment content.
findings.location: What section: 'Enrich the db-migrations overlay prompt with dso content'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, replace 'dso content' with either: (a) a direct enumeration of the recall patterns and FP-guard conditions in the plan body, or (b) a concrete repo path (e.g., `docs/adr/NNNN-db-migrations-rubric.md`) that the executor can `read_file`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete file path for the new eval spec (e.g., `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-db-migrations.eval.yaml`) in the Files section so the artifact lands where `load_eval_spec('code-review-db-migrations')` can resolve it.
findings.finding: The plan's second file artifact ('eval spec + fixtures for the db-migrations overlay') does not exist at any discoverable path in the repository, and the plan does not specify the concrete file path to be created.
findings.location: Files section: 'eval spec + fixtures for the db-migrations overlay (recall + no-fire cases)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-db-migrations.eval.yaml` as the explicit path for the eval spec in the Files section, following the naming convention of existing specs such as `completion-verifier.eval.yaml`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete output path for the db-migrations overlay eval spec (e.g., `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-db-migrations.eval.yaml`) and confirm or establish the eval schema/harness that will execute it.
findings.finding: The plan's second named deliverable — 'eval spec + fixtures for the db-migrations overlay (recall + no-fire cases)' — has no existing file in the repo, no concrete output path specified, and no precedent format for a code-review overlay eval spec (the `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/` directory contains only plan-review, ticket-quality, and completion-verifier eval specs, not any code-review overlay eval). An implementing agent must invent both the path and the schema.
findings.location: Files section: 'eval spec + fixtures for the db-migrations overlay (recall + no-fire cases)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a unit test file (e.g., `tests/unit/test_code_review_evals.py` or extend `test_new_eval_specs.py`) that calls `load_eval_spec('code-review-db-migrations')`, asserts `validate_eval_spec(strict=True) == []`, and checks the dataset has both recall and no-fire cases — matching the pattern in `test_new_eval_specs.py`.
findings.finding: The plan does not enumerate a unit test file to pin the new eval spec's shape (analogous to `test_new_eval_specs.py`), yet the established repo pattern requires a companion offline test that calls `load_eval_spec('code-review-db-migrations')` to guard spec validity, scorer registration, and dataset balance.
findings.location: Files section; AC2: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
